title: SMSociety 2026 Conference Programme – #SMSociety 2026
snippet: Conference program listing Fabio Giglietto as an expert panelist for the 'Meta Content Library Community Roundtable: Where to for Social Media Research?' at the International Conference on Social Media and Society (SMSociety 2026) in Glasgow, discussing data access challenges under the European Union's Digital Services Act.
description: Fabio Giglietto is featured as a panelist for the 'Meta Content Library Community Roundtable' at the 14th International Conference on Social Media & Society, discussing social media research data access.

title: Indagine urbinate: "Così l’algoritmo influenza il voto" - Il Resto del Carlino
description: News article from Il Resto del Carlino

title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: This media article details Giglietto's research on the mechanisms of Meta's content distribution. It describes the 72% reach reduction suffered by mainstream Italian politicians, the corresponding rise in visibility for conspiratorial sources, and the regulatory reactions from the European Commission investigating Meta's systemic risk mitigation.
description: An article discussing the political and public fallout of Professor Giglietto's research on Meta's algorithms, including its feature on Rai3's 'Report' and subsequent European Parliament inquiries.

title: La schedatura dei docenti, l’antifascismo e il tarlo della scuola del ’68 - Centro Studi Sereno Regis
description: News article from Centro Studi Sereno Regis

title: L'articolo 21 nella gabbia di Facebook
snippet: An analytical piece on digital free speech and constitutional rights in Italy that heavily cites Fabio Giglietto’s empirical research, pointing to his findings on the 72% decrease in Facebook visibility for Italian parliamentarians.
description: An opinion piece analyzing digital free speech in Italy that heavily cites Fabio Giglietto's research on the impacts of Facebook's political content reduction policy.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews
Meno contenuti politici nei feed, la stretta di Meta è stata meno efficace contro gli estremisti - Il Sole 24 ORE

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: An investigative report detailing how Fabio Giglietto's study, which analyzed over 2.5 million Facebook posts, prompted Italian parliamentarians to seek bipartisan legislation against social media algorithms that suppress institutional channels while boosting extremism.
description: An article detailing a research study co-authored by Fabio Giglietto on how Meta's algorithmic changes affect the organic reach of political groups in Italy.
